section1.title: What is performance and SEO optimization?
section1.p1: Page load speed and search rankings directly affect conversions and revenue. According to Google, a 1-second delay reduces conversions by 7%, and 53% of users abandon a page that takes more than 3 seconds to load.
section1.p2: Performance optimization is a set of technical improvements that speed up page loading, enhance user experience, and boost a site's position in search engines. Google officially includes Core Web Vitals as a ranking factor — a technically weak site loses ground to competitors even with great content.

section2.title: When does your site need optimization?
items: PageSpeed Insights score is below 70 on mobile
Pages take more than 3 seconds to load
Core Web Vitals show yellow or red metrics
Search rankings are declining or stagnant for no clear reason
High bounce rate — users leave immediately
Heavy images, third-party scripts, or outdated plugins are slowing things down
The site doesn't use caching or a CDN
section2.footer: If at least two points apply — it's time for an audit.

section3.title: What's included?
items: Image optimization and conversion to modern formats (WebP, AVIF)
CSS, JavaScript, and HTML minification and bundling
Core Web Vitals improvement: LCP, INP, CLS
Technical SEO: meta tags, canonical, robots.txt, sitemap

section4.title: How we work
section4.p1: We start with diagnostics: measuring current performance through PageSpeed Insights, Lighthouse, GTmetrix, and Google Search Console. We identify bottlenecks — from heavy images and redundant scripts to server response time issues.
section4.p2: Optimization is done in stages, prioritizing the changes that deliver the highest impact. All improvements are verified on real devices and documented in a comparison report.
section4.p3: After the work is done, we provide maintenance recommendations and can set up ongoing metrics monitoring if needed.

section5.title: Tools and technology
section5.p1: Google Lighthouse, PageSpeed Insights, GTmetrix, WebPageTest, Screaming Frog, Google Search Console — we use professional tools for precise diagnostics and measurable results.
section5.p2: We work with any stack: WordPress, Webflow, Bitrix, and custom projects built on Vue / Nuxt or React / Next.js. No one-size-fits-all approach — only targeted solutions suited to your architecture.

speedComparison.subtitle: Compare the approaches and choose the one that fits your goals.

speedFaq.a: Core Web Vitals are Google's metrics for measuring user experience: LCP (how fast the main content loads), INP (responsiveness to user interaction), and CLS (visual layout stability). These scores directly affect search rankings.

speedFaq.q: How much can PageSpeed realistically improve?
speedFaq.a: In most cases, PageSpeed Score improves by 20–50 points after comprehensive optimization. The exact gain depends on the current state of the site, its architecture, and the technology stack.

Yes. Google officially factors Core Web Vitals into rankings. A slow site loses ground to competitors even with equally good content. Speed also affects bounce rate, which indirectly impacts SEO further.

speedFaq.q: Do you only work with WordPress?
speedFaq.a: No. We work with any CMS and stack: WordPress, Webflow, Bitrix, and custom projects on Vue / Nuxt or React / Next.js. Tools and approach are tailored to your specific architecture.

speedFaq.q: What's included in a technical SEO audit?
speedFaq.a: We check meta tags, headings, canonical tags, robots.txt, sitemaps, structured data, internal linking, broken links, redirects, and page indexing in Google Search Console.

speedFaq.q: How soon will results be visible?
speedFaq.a: Technical work takes 3 to 14 days. PageSpeed Score improvements are visible immediately after optimization. Search ranking gains are gradual — typically noticeable 2–6 weeks after technical changes are applied.

speedFaq.q: Does the site design need to change?
speedFaq.a: No. Performance and SEO optimization is purely technical work that doesn't affect the visual appearance of your site. We work under the hood.
Cost depends on the audit type, scope of work, and any add-ons. Use our calculator for an estimate, or contact us for a precise quote.
